findings.description: N+1 query: low_stock_report() runs one movements COUNT(*) per low-stock product inside a Python loop (K+1 round-trips); replace with a single correlated-subquery/JOIN query.
Entire products table fetched with fetchall() and the stock <= reorder_level filter applied in Python instead of SQL, wasting memory and transfer proportional to catalog size.
A new sqlite3 connection is opened and closed for every method call (no pooling or long-lived connection), paying file-open/schema-parse cost and discarding the page cache on each hot-path operation.
search_by_name() has no LIMIT and uses a leading-wildcard LIKE that forces a full table scan, materializing an unbounded list of Product objects; add LIMIT/pagination or FTS.

Each reserve/restock write is its own commit (full fsync) under the default rollback journal with no batch API, capping pipeline write throughput at disk fsync rate; enable WAL and add bulk operations.
If movements(sku, created_at) lacks a composite index (schema not in scope, unverifiable), each per-product COUNT is a full scan of movements, multiplying the N+1 cost; verify and index.
fragment is f-string-interpolated into the LIKE clause (SQL injection, also defeats prepared-statement reuse); noted briefly, detail deferred to critic-security.

reserve() is a non-atomic check-then-act (SELECT then UPDATE in separate statements), so concurrent reservations can oversell; a single conditional UPDATE with rowcount check fixes it and halves round-trips; detail deferred to critic-logic.
Off-by-one: stock > quantity should be stock >= quantity, making the last available unit unreservable; detail deferred to critic-logic.
try/finally with manual close() in every method could use contextlib.closing or a shared context-manager helper; purely stylistic.
